followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: The PDF confirms my prior radiation A_r-factor objection, so I keep that as a real major issue rather than adopting the other reviewers' higher technical-soundness assessment. I lower the overall score modestly because the other reviews correctly emphasize the very low research novelty, but I reject their claim that the radiation affine formula is fully correct.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The claim that the radiation-era exact integral and asymptotics are fully correct misses the printed A_r-dependent coefficients; setting A_r=1 in a figure does not fix the general equations.
The objection that the matter-era antiderivative lacks bounds or special-function form is not a serious technical problem; the paper only claims it is not elementary and uses correct asymptotics.
The HTML-rendering concerns about missing dimension labels are artifacts: the PDF labels the sections as 1+1 and 3+1.
The curvature formulas are readable in the PDF and check out under the stated sign convention, so they should not remain an unresolved objection.
The no-technical-objections review overlooks the radiation coefficient error, the p=0 de Sitter omission, and the overgeneral wording of the affine formalism.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The other reviewers are right that the paper is best understood as an educational synthesis with very low research novelty.
The point that 1+1 Einstein dynamics cannot produce the usual Friedmann radiation and matter powers is valid, though the paper's conclusion partially acknowledges the kinematical nature of the model.
Their checks of the matter-era asymptotic powers and the nonzero-p de Sitter hyperbola derivation agree with my assessment.
The de Sitter negative-eta branch could be explained more pedagogically, although it is not a technical flaw.

main_claims.claim: Radiation, matter, and exact de Sitter benchmark scale factors become respectively a(eta)=A_r eta, a(eta)=A_m eta^2, and a(eta)=-ell/eta on the chosen conformal-time branches.
main_claims.notes: The transformations follow from integrating d eta=c dt/a(t) using the usual 3+1 FRW power laws as kinematical inputs for the 1+1 discussion.

main_claims.claim: The 1+1 homogeneous conformal metric has the listed Christoffel symbols and geodesic first integrals p=a^2 x' and a^2(eta'^2-x'^2)=kappa.
main_claims.notes: These are standard consequences of homogeneity in x and affine normalization. Branch signs and some degenerate cases remain under-discussed.

main_claims.claim: Null geodesics are straight lines in conformal coordinates.
main_claims.notes: For nondegenerate null geodesics with p nonzero, dx/deta=+-1 follows immediately.

main_claims.claim: The radiation-era timelike affine parameter has a cubic small-eta regime and quadratic large-eta regime.
main_claims.notes: The qualitative powers and crossover condition A_r eta ~ |p| are right, but the printed exact integral and asymptotic coefficients are missing powers of A_r.
main_claims.paper_location: Section III.2, Eqs. (16)-(20); Section VI around Eq. (48)

main_claims.claim: The matter-era timelike affine parameter has quintic small-eta and cubic large-eta asymptotics.
main_claims.notes: The A_m-dependent coefficients in the displayed asymptotics follow from d lambda/d eta=A_m^2 eta^4/sqrt(p^2+A_m^2 eta^4).
main_claims.paper_location: Section III.3, Eqs. (21)-(24); Section VI, Eqs. (49)-(50)

main_claims.claim: Timelike geodesics in the exact de Sitter flat patch are hyperbolae in the (x,u=-eta) plane.
main_claims.notes: The derivation is correct for p nonzero. The p=0 comoving timelike geodesic is excluded by formulas dividing by p and is not represented by a finite hyperbola.

main_claims.claim: The 1+1 scalar curvature formula gives nonzero radiation and matter curvature and constant de Sitter curvature under the stated Riemann sign convention.
main_claims.notes: The PDF formulas are readable and agree with a direct substitution into R=2(a'^2-aa'')/a^4 for the stated convention.
main_claims.paper_location: Section IV, Eqs. (29)-(33); Appendix A, Eqs. (A6)-(A7)

main_claims.claim: The affine-parameter integral is a general formalism for spatially flat conformal metrics.
main_claims.notes: It is general for homogeneous FRW conformal factors a(eta). It is not general for an arbitrary 1+1 conformal factor f(eta,x), where x is not cyclic and p is not conserved.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Section VI, Eqs. (42)-(43); Appendix A

missing_assumptions.assumption: A_r and A_m are positive and eta is restricted to branches where a(eta)>0.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The asymptotic signs and physical interpretation of early and late conformal time depend on these branch choices.

missing_assumptions.assumption: p is nonzero whenever formulas divide by p or |p|.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Section III.2, Eq. (18); Section III.4, Eqs. (27)-(28); Section VI, Eq. (47)
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The p=0 timelike case and the degenerate null case are not represented by several displayed formulas.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Radiation and matter scale factors are 3+1 FRW inputs used kinematically in the 1+1 model.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this, the examples could be mistaken for self-contained 1+1 Einstein-matter cosmologies.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The affine-parameter formula in Eq. (43) is intended only for homogeneous conformal factors a(eta).
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Spatial dependence in f(eta,x) destroys the cyclic-coordinate argument used to conserve p.

missing_assumptions.assumption: For spacelike geodesics or analytic continuations, p^2+kappa a^2 must remain nonnegative on the interval considered.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Otherwise the displayed real affine integrals are not defined.

novelty_assessment: The manuscript is primarily pedagogical. The conformal-time scale factors, homogeneous conformal-metric first integrals, null straight-line result, de Sitter flat-patch hyperbolae, and curvature formulas are all standard textbook-level material. Its useful contribution is comparative organization and some interpretive cautions about exact de Sitter versus generic vacuum domination, but I do not see a new theorem, method, or research-level result.
score_rationale: Most of the standard geodesic and curvature material is correct, but a central worked radiation example has wrong A_r-dependent coefficients, and the presentation overstates generality in places. Because the paper is essentially pedagogical and low-novelty, these fixable but real issues keep it below the competent routine range for a research venue.
summary: This remains a short pedagogical survey of conformal time, geodesics, affine parameters, and curvature for spatially flat FRW-like examples, mostly in 1+1 dimensions. I do not certify the whole proof. After reading the other reviewers and checking the PDF equations, I retain my main concern: the radiation-era affine-parameter formula has incorrect A_r factors in the printed exact expression and in both asymptotic coefficients. Most other core calculations are standard and locally plausible or checkable, but the paper is low-novelty and has several overgenerality and edge-case issues.

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (16) gives d lambda/d eta=A_r^2 eta^2/sqrt(p^2+A_r^2 eta^2). Direct integration gives lambda-lambda0=eta sqrt(p^2+A_r^2 eta^2)/2 - p^2 asinh(A_r eta/|p|)/(2 A_r), so the limits are A_r^2 eta^3/(3|p|) and A_r eta^2/2. The paper's Eq. (17) instead differentiates to eta^2/sqrt(p^2+A_r^2 eta^2), and Eqs. (19)-(20) give eta^3/(3|p|) and eta^2/(2 A_r).
technical_objections.objection: The radiation-era affine-parameter integral has the wrong powers of A_r.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section III.2, Eqs. (16)-(20); Section VI around Eq. (48)

technical_objections.evidence: The derivation requires x-translation invariance and p=a^2 x' conserved. Appendix A explicitly allows f(eta,x), for which partial_x f terms enter the geodesic equations and the same conserved quantity is unavailable.
technical_objections.objection: The stated generality of the affine formalism is too broad if read as applying to arbitrary conformal factors.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract; Section VI, Eqs. (42)-(43); Appendix A

technical_objections.evidence: In 1+1 Einstein gravity the Einstein tensor is identically trivial, so the usual radiation and matter Friedmann powers are not derived in the toy model. The conclusion partly mitigates this by calling the treatment kinematical, but the benchmark section itself can still mislead a pedagogical reader.
technical_objections.objection: The radiation and matter labels should be explicitly framed at the point of use as imported 3+1 FRW dynamics, not 1+1 Einstein dynamics.

technical_objections.evidence: Eqs. (27)-(28) divide by p and define b=ell/|p|. For p=0, dx/du=0 and the geodesic is comoving with x constant, not a finite hyperbola.
technical_objections.objection: The de Sitter hyperbola statement omits the p=0 timelike case.

technical_objections.evidence: The square root p^2+kappa a^2 must be real, the null formula requires p nonzero for a nondegenerate affine parameter, and the sign choice in d eta/d lambda is mostly suppressed after the first-integral derivation.
technical_objections.objection: Domain, sign, and branch conditions for the affine formulas are under-specified.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section III, Eq. (14); Section VI, Eq. (43)
